stories.title: All-Star Week Opens in Philadelphia — HBCU Swingman Classic Tonight

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
All-Star Week is HERE.

Philadelphia hasn't hosted since 1976. That's 50 years.

This week: Draft (Saturday). HRD (Monday). All-Star Game (Tuesday).

The city waited half a century for this.

#AllStarWeek #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The HBCU Swingman Classic is TONIGHT.

7:00 PM ET at Citizens Bank Park. On MLB Network.

50 players. 19 HBCUs. Jimmy Rollins and Ricky Weeks managing.

First pitch from Philadelphia Mayor Cherelle Parker.

#AllStarWeek #MLB

article.headline: All-Star Week Is Here: The HBCU Swingman Classic Opens Philadelphia's 50-Year Wait

stories.title: Home Run Derby — Harper Confirmed at Home, 6 of 8 Set, Schwarber TBD

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Bryce Harper is in the Home Run Derby.

The 2018 champ gets to swing in front of his home crowd at Citizens Bank Park on Monday night.

Six of eight spots confirmed. Two still open.

Derby. July 13. Netflix. 8 PM ET.

#MLB #HomeRunDerby #Phillies

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber has 31 home runs. He leads all of MLB.

He's a two-time Derby participant. The Derby is at HIS ballpark.

Don Mattingly thought he'd do it. Schwarber hasn't officially confirmed.

Decision expected soon.

#MLB #HomeRunDerby #Phillies

article.headline: Bryce Harper Is In, Kyle Schwarber Is TBD, and the Home Run Derby Field Is Almost Set

stories.title: MLB Draft TOMORROW — Cholowsky vs. Emerson to the Wire at No. 1

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The MLB Draft is TOMORROW.

White Sox have the No. 1 pick. The finalists: Roch Cholowsky (UCLA SS), Grady Emerson (prep SS), Vahn Lackey (Georgia Tech C).

Essentially a coin flip between Cholowsky and Emerson.

1:00 PM ET. NBC/Peacock.

#MLBDraft #WhiteSox

article.headline: The MLB Draft Is Tomorrow and the No. 1 Pick Is Still Undecided

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tyler Tolbert tied an MLB record that stood since 1952.

The Royals outfielder had hits in 12 straight plate appearances — joining Johnny Kling, Pinky Higgins, Walt Dropo, and Jose Miranda.

His bat goes to Cooperstown.

#Royals #MLB

article.headline: Tyler Tolbert Tied an MLB Record That Stood Since 1952 — Then Flew Out in the 9th

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:

- **Type:** MLB record graphic
- **Concept:** "MLB RECORD TIED" banner in gold. "12 Consecutive Hits." Horizontal timeline strip: Kling 1902 · Higgins 1938 · Dropo 1952 · Miranda 2024 · Tolbert 2026. "Bat Going to Cooperstown" badge in lower corner.
- **Imagn search terms:** `Tyler Tolbert Kansas City Royals`

stories.title: Skubal Trade Watch — 24 Days to the August 3 Deadline

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal. 24 days to the August 3 trade deadline.

The Tigers ace is the most coveted arm on the market. The Braves are the frontrunner.

Detroit is playing its best baseball since June. The price isn't dropping.

#MLB #Tigers

article.headline: 24 Days to the Deadline: Why the Braves Are Still the Team to Beat for Tarik Skubal
